creator: mk@elxsi.de
text:
The system runs without any problems for some weeks and than it crashed.
After rebooting the system it sometimes only takes about 60 seconds and
the system crashes again. This repeats for some time (e.g. some reboots) 
nd than the system runs fine for some more weeks.

Please note, it doesn't seem to be a hardware problem, as I installed
freebsd on another system with completly other hardware (another
mainboard/ram/cpu/harddrives/controllers). The first system was a compaq
proliant server and the second system just a normal PC. I also confirmed
that we don't have a power issue (used different safety switches and
USVs). The bug report applies to both systems.

I tried recompiling the kernel with DBD and to create a kernel dump,
but I'm unable to enter the debugger when the system hangs. No kernel
dumps get created.

I have disabled ACPI and the apache accf stuff, and I added the following
stuff to /boot/loader.conf:

accf_http_load="NO"
accf_data_load="NO"
hint.apic.0.disabled="1"
hint.acpi.0.disabled="1"
kern.ipc.nmbclusters="0"


I'm running apache22 (installed via the freebsd ports), postfix (also
via ports) , spamassassin, mysql and bind, nothing else special.

Here are some debug information:
